---
title: "[DEPRECATED] Manyfold Home Assistant Add-on"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-copy: true
    theme: cosmo
    embed-resources: false
---

> **This file is deprecated.** It documents the previous Manyfold add-on that this repository was originally based on. Kept as a structural reference only.

![Manyfold Home Assistant Add-on logo](manyfold_solo/logo.png){fig-alt="Manyfold Home Assistant Add-on logo" width="220"}

## Overview

This project provides a **Home Assistant OS add-on** for **Manyfold** (3D model manager), packaged around the `manyfold-solo` image with persistent storage and configurable host-backed library/index paths.

It is designed for Home Assistant users who want to run Manyfold locally and manage 3D print files (STL, 3MF, and related assets) with a simple add-on install flow.

- Add-on name: `Manyfold`
- Slug: `manyfold_solo`
- Web UI port: `3214`
- Supported architectures: `amd64`, `aarch64`

Primary documentation for Manyfold itself: [manyfold.app/get-started](https://manyfold.app/get-started/)

## Features

- Runs Manyfold on port `3214`
- Persists app data, database, cache, and settings under Home Assistant add-on config storage (`/config`)
- Supports configurable library and thumbnails/index paths on Home Assistant host storage
- Validates configured paths and refuses startup when paths resolve outside allowed roots (`/share`, `/media`, `/config`)
- No external PostgreSQL or Redis required for this add-on setup
- Supports `amd64` and `aarch64`
- Works with Home Assistant add-on repository flow

## Repository Structure

- `repository.yaml`: Home Assistant add-on repository manifest
- `manyfold_solo/`: Add-on package consumed by Home Assistant OS
- `manyfold_solo/config.yaml`: Add-on metadata, schema, defaults, ports, and options
- `manyfold_solo/build.yaml`: Upstream image digests per architecture
- `manyfold_solo/rootfs/`: Runtime scripts and service definitions for the containerized add-on

## Default Paths

The add-on defaults are configured for a first-run setup:

- Library path: `/share/manyfold/models`
- Thumbnails path: `/config/thumbnails`

## Installation (Home Assistant Add-on Store)

1. Open **Home Assistant**.
2. Go to **Settings -> Add-ons -> Add-on Store**.
3. Open the menu (`...`) and choose **Repositories**.
4. Add your add-on repository URL:
   - `https://github.com/ToledoEM/hassio-addons`
5. Refresh the Add-on Store.
6. Install **Manyfold**.
7. Configure the add-on (the defaults are safe to start):
   - `library_path: /share/manyfold/models`
   - `thumbnails_path: /config/thumbnails`
   - `secret_key_base: ""` (leave blank to auto-generate and persist)
   - `puid` / `pgid` (set to a non-root user/group if needed)
8. Start the add-on.
9. Open the web UI at:
   - `http://<HOME_ASSISTANT_IP>:3214`

## Local Add-on Development (on HA Host)

If you are testing directly on a Home Assistant host:

1. Copy `manyfold_solo/` into `/addons/manyfold_solo` on the host.
2. In the Add-on Store menu (`...`), click **Check for updates**.
3. Install and run the local add-on.

## Library and Index Workflow

1. Place your model files (`.stl`, `.3mf`, archives, etc.) into the configured library folder on the host.
   - Default example: `/share/manyfold/models`
2. In the Manyfold UI, configure a library that points to the same path exposed in the container.
3. Manyfold scans and indexes files.
4. Thumbnails and indexing artifacts are persisted in:
   - `/config/thumbnails` (default)

## Add-on Configuration Options

The add-on supports the following configurable options (defined in `manyfold_solo/config.yaml`):

- `secret_key_base`: App secret (auto-generated when empty and persisted)
- `puid`: User ID used for file ownership/permissions
- `pgid`: Group ID used for file ownership/permissions
- `multiuser`: Enable or disable Manyfold multi-user mode
- `library_path`: Library path to scan/index
- `thumbnails_path`: Persistent thumbnails/index artifacts path (must be under `/config`)
- `log_level`: `info`, `debug`, `warn`, `error`
- `web_concurrency`: Puma worker process count
- `rails_max_threads`: Max threads per Puma worker
- `default_worker_concurrency`: Sidekiq default queue concurrency
- `performance_worker_concurrency`: Sidekiq performance queue concurrency
- `max_file_upload_size`: Maximum uploaded archive size (bytes)
- `max_file_extract_size`: Maximum extracted archive size (bytes)

## Example Configuration

```yaml
secret_key_base: ""
puid: 1000
pgid: 1000
multiuser: true
library_path: /share/manyfold/models
thumbnails_path: /config/thumbnails
log_level: info
web_concurrency: 4
rails_max_threads: 16
default_worker_concurrency: 4
performance_worker_concurrency: 1
max_file_upload_size: 1073741824
max_file_extract_size: 1073741824
```

## Validation and Safety Behavior

The add-on performs path validation at startup:

- `library_path` and `thumbnails_path` must resolve inside allowed mapped roots
- Allowed roots are `/share`, `/media`, and `/config`
- `thumbnails_path` must resolve under `/config` to ensure persistent storage
- Startup fails if the configured library path is invalid or inaccessible

## Networking

- Web UI listens on port `3214`
- Home Assistant add-on port mapping exposes `3214/tcp`
- Default web UI URL pattern: `http://[HOST]:[PORT:3214]`

## License

This repository is licensed under the MIT License. See `LICENSE` for details.
